compiled_state.title: As a DSO practitioner, the Deep Sonnet B reviewer applies deep verification checks evaluating test quality and coverage

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Enhance the Deep Sonnet B verification specialist (code-reviewer-deep-verification.md) with researched sub-criteria for test quality, edge case coverage, and mock correctness — tuned to this project's testing patterns.

**Why**: The verification specialist evaluates test quality for high-risk changes. Its generic checklist doesn't account for this project's test conventions (bash assert.sh helpers, pytest fixtures, .test-index RED markers, test-batched.sh patterns).

**Scope**:
- IN: Updating verification-focused checklist items with project-aware test patterns; adding sub-criteria for bash test quality (assert_eq/assert_contains usage, cleanup traps); researching verification best practices for bash/Python test suites
- OUT: Modifying correctness or hygiene dimensions; changing the output contract

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the Deep Verification reviewer includes sub-criteria that recognize this project's test patterns (bash assert.sh helpers, pytest parametrize, .test-index associations) and flag violations of those patterns
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, mock correctness sub-criteria include checks for over-mocking (mocking the unit under test) and under-mocking (calling real external resources in unit tests)
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'
- When this story is complete, unit tests verify the updated agent file structure
  ← Satisfies: 'Review Intelligence & Precision'

## Considerations

- [Testing] Ironically, the verification reviewer's own improvements need verification — test against sample review outputs
- [Maintainability] Sub-criteria must not be so project-specific that they break portability to consuming projects
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Fixed: Enhanced Deep verification reviewer with project test patterns, mock correctness, TDD awareness
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
